title: newgidmap: executable not found in $PATH on Ubuntu 18.04
body:
**Description**
I installed buildah via the Ubuntu PPA, following the instructions on https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/install.md#ubuntu

It installed successfully, but when I run `buildah` i get this error:

```
Error: error running newgidmap: exec: "newgidmap": executable file not found in $PATH:
ERRO[0000] error running newgidmap: : exec: "newgidmap": executable file not found in $PATH
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/bionic,now 1.8-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa22 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.8-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
Git Commit:
Built:           Fri Apr 12 19:13:55 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.2 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.2 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.2 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux halite 4.15.0-47-generic #50-Ubuntu SMP Wed Mar 13 10:44:52 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body: @lsm5 Do we need a recommends newgid?
The package is `uidmap`, and it's a dependency, not a recommends, because the bud command is non-functional without it.
I'll make that change, btw, I'm working on snap packages for these tools, so hopefully the PPA can be abandoned soon.
this is required only for rootless builds, root still works without it.  Should it still be a dependency and not a recommends?
I updated to the latest and it looks like it all works. Feel free to close this issue unless you want to use it track further work.

Thanks for the update! Also @giuseppe yes, a depends, because a major part of the program doesn't work without it on the local machine. Recommends are for things like a GUI that talks to the daemon, or a database server that the program can talk to, but also might exist on some other box.
